pr-510.title: hotfix(rpc-s1): fix syntax error breaking every repo-connect button
pr-510.rootCause: missing closing brace in rpcSubmitCreate/rpcSubmitConnect's try block broke the ENTIRE inline <script> tag, silently making every button handler undefined
pr-510.regressionTest: IT4 in tests/check-rpc-s1-connect-repo.js — vm.Script-compiles the extracted inline script body

pr-511.title: hotfix: fix JSONB rollup fields crashing product view on real sync
pr-511.rootCause: _renderProductView called JSON.parse() unconditionally on 5 JSONB columns (health_counts, test_coverage, ac_coverage, taxonomy, dod_status_counts); pg auto-parses JSONB to objects, so this crashed with SyntaxError: "[object Object]" is not valid JSON the first time any product completed a real sync round-trip on staging
pr-511.fix: added _parseJsonbField() helper in products.js, only parses if value is actually a string
pr-511.regressionTest: new test in tests/check-pr-s2-products-route.js using object-shaped (real pg) mock values end-to-end through handleGetProductView

pr-513.title: hotfix(pr-s5..pr-s7): fall back to story.id when story.slug is absent in rollup breakdowns
pr-513.status: OPEN, CI initially failed on an unrelated pre-existing check-workspace-state.js schema break introduced by this same session's earlier state.json rewrite (fixed in a follow-up commit on this branch, see below) — re-check CI before merging
pr-513.rootCause: computeTestCoverageRollup/computeAcCoverageRollup/computeTaxonomyRollup in product-rollup.js pushed slug: story.slug with no fallback; 17 real stories in this repo's pipeline-state.json have id but no slug
pr-513.fix: all 3 call sites now use story.slug || story.id
pr-513.regressionTest: 3 new tests in tests/check-pr-s2-product-rollup.js, one per function; also confirmed 0 blank entries against a real read of this repo's own pipeline-state.json (was 17)

NOT YET DONE for PR #510, #511, or #513 — flagged as a pending action below.

openFindings.summary: Test-coverage and AC-coverage per-feature lists show blank ': 100%' entries with no story name
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED — computeTestCoverageRollup() and computeAcCoverageRollup() in src/web-ui/modules/product-rollup.js pushed `slug: story.slug` with no fallback. 17 real stories in this repo's own .github/pipeline-state.json (e.g. ougl.1 through ougl.9) have an `id` field but no `slug` field.
openFindings.fix: APPLIED, MERGED (PR #513), AND DEPLOYED to wuce-staging.
openFindings.status: CLOSED — user confirmed live: all previously-blank entries (ougl.1-7, sri.1-3, etc.) now render their real story id as the label.

openFindings.summary: /admin/credits returns {"error":"Forbidden"} for the account owner (heymishy) on wuce-staging
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED — admin role is seeded via ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS env var; this var was never set on wuce-staging.
openFindings.fix: APPLIED AND DEPLOYED — `flyctl secrets set ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS=heymishy --app wuce-staging`, confirmed via boot log `[arl-s4] admin role seeded for 1 login(s)`.
openFindings.status: CLOSED — user confirmed live: /admin/credits is now accessible.

openFindings.summary: Some epic names render garbled, e.g. 'Governance extractability ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â enforcement selection'
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED NOT a rendering/code bug — the mangled string is baked into the SOURCE DATA itself in .github/pipeline-state.json (double-encoded UTF-8).
openFindings.fix: Not a code fix — would require a data cleanup pass on the stored feature.name strings in pipeline-state.json. Cosmetic, low priority.
openFindings.status: OPEN — still needs a user decision (do it / leave it), not actioned

openFindings.summary: The per-feature test-coverage/AC-coverage list is a single unsorted flat list of 115+ story codes with no grouping — very hard to read, unlike the properly-grouped 'Epics' section rendered further down the same page
openFindings.rootCause: Not a bug — a design/UX gap. The Epics section already groups by epic via computeTaxonomyRollup(); the per-feature coverage breakdown does not reuse that grouping.
openFindings.fix: Needs a design decision, not a hotfix — e.g. group the per-feature breakdown by epic the same way, or collapse to just the blended % with an expandable details view for the full per-story list.
openFindings.status: OPEN — flagged as design debt, still needs a user priority/scope decision

openFindings.summary: Clicking 'New feature' routes correctly to a skill-session chat, but the skill invocation fails with a generic 'Error — please try again' in the chat UI
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED — got the real sse_error log line: `"Copilot API HTTP 403: unauthorized: not licensed to use Copilot"`. wuce-staging had SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER set to a non-default value (src/modules/skill-turn-executor.js routes to either 'anthropic' [default] or 'copilot' based on this env var), causing every skill turn to attempt the GitHub Copilot Chat Completions API path instead of the Anthropic Messages API — and the GitHub token/account in use has no Copilot license.
openFindings.fix: APPLIED AND DEPLOYED — user chose the 'just fix the staging config' option (not removing Copilot support from the codebase, which would be a separate story). Ran `flyctl secrets unset SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER --app wuce-staging`, confirmed removed from `flyctl secrets list`, confirmed ANTHROPIC_API_KEY still present. This makes the code fall back to its 'anthropic' default (skill-turn-executor.js line 616/659/681: `(process.env.SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER || 'anthropic')`).
openFindings.status: FIX DEPLOYED, NOT YET RE-TESTED — user has not yet retried 'New feature' since the unset; this is the next thing to confirm

checkpoint.contextAtWrite: Continuation of the live-staging-verification thread. PR #513 (F1 fix) merged clean after the check-workspace-state.js schema issue was fixed; staging redeployed via `flyctl deploy` and confirmed serving. User confirmed BOTH F1 (blank slugs) and F2 (/admin/credits) are fixed live. User then retried 'New feature' and hit the model error again, but this time pasted the actual fly log excerpt containing the real `sse_error` line: `"Copilot API HTTP 403: unauthorized: not licensed to use Copilot"`. Root cause: src/modules/skill-turn-executor.js routes every skill turn to either the Anthropic Messages API or the GitHub Copilot Chat Completions API based on the SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER env var (default 'anthropic' if unset) — wuce-staging had this var explicitly set (confirmed present in `flyctl secrets list` from earlier in the session, though the value itself is never visible via flyctl), forcing every turn through the Copilot path with a GitHub token that has no Copilot license. User was asked whether to (a) just fix the staging config, or (b) remove Copilot support from the codebase entirely as a proper short-track story; chose (a). Fixed via `flyctl secrets unset SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER --app wuce-staging` — confirmed removed from the secrets list, confirmed ANTHROPIC_API_KEY still present, confirmed the app redeployed cleanly and is serving 200s. NOT YET RE-TESTED by the user since this unset.
checkpoint.resumeInstruction: Read this entire state.json first, then workspace/learnings.md's relevant entries for full narrative context — do not rely on any prior conversation history, assume none carries over. Next concrete steps, in priority order: (1) Ask the user to retry 'New feature' on wuce-staging now that SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER has been unset — this is the single most important thing to confirm, since F5's fix is deployed but not yet verified working. (2) If it still fails, get a fresh sse_error log line — the failure mode will be different now (should be a real Anthropic API error if anything, not the Copilot 403) and needs fresh diagnosis, not a repeat of the same root cause. (3) F3 (mojibake data in pipeline-state.json) and F4 (flat coverage-list design) both still need a user decision on priority/scope — do not unilaterally act on either. (4) Separately, PR #510, #511, and #513 were all shipped as fast hotfixes without full short-track story artefacts — technically needs a retrospective-story.md per CLAUDE.md's Artefact-first rule remedy; raise at a natural pause, non-blocking. Also worth a brief retrospective-note on the SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER config-only fix (no code changed, so likely does not need a full artefact chain, but flag it). (5) Consider whether a startup-time env var sanity check (warn if SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER=copilot but no Copilot-scoped token is configured, or similar) is worth proposing given this is now the 4th silently-misconfigured-env-var incident this session (PLATFORM_TENANT_ID, ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS, POSTHOG_KEY_STAGING/PROD, and now SKILL_EXECUTOR_PROVIDER) — see workspace/learnings.md's 'Silently-optional env vars' entry, which should be extended to mention this 4th instance is actually the inverse case (an explicitly-set-but-wrong value, not a missing one) so the proposed startup check needs to cover both directions.
